programmes.show_description: Philadelphia, PA. Casey Webb is in Philadelphia for the epic Classic Whale Challenge, a 13-pound bagel sandwich stuffed with cream cheese, salmon, whitefish, tomatoes and onions. S2, Ep5
Seattle, WA. Casey Webb travels to Seattle where he climbs a Mount Fuji-sized burger and takes on seven terrifyingly spicy Buffalo wings that have defeated over 5,000 challengers. S2, Ep6
Hittin' The Grill. Guy Fieri is sampling foods straight off the grill. In Tacoma, the pub grub grill menu includes a take on killer kabobs and homegrown beet salads. S16, Ep10
Unlikely Partners. Guy Fieri is checking out some unique combos. In Los Angeles's Korea Town, a craft beer joint is putting duck in the fries and in the French dip. S16, Ep11
Teleshopping.

Teleshopping.
Rick Stein visits Yorkshire's largest rhubarb growing area, and then bakes his favourite dessert - a rhubarb crumble. Plus, he makes a delicious rabbit pie. S1, Ep7
Rick Stein explores Wales' amazing produce. He cooks a rice pilaf with Welsh mussels. Plus, he visits a salt marsh lamb farm in North Wales. S1, Ep8
Rick Stein goes on a pigeon shoot in Cornwall, inspiring him to cook pigeon and peas. Plus, after visiting Devon, he makes a warm skate salad. S1, Ep1
Rick Stein cooks sautéed chicken with 40 cloves of garlic and a warm pheasant salad. Plus, he learns how cider is made at an old-fashioned cider farm in Somerset. S1, Ep2
Cheap And Easy. Ree makes cheap and easy dishes that are perfect for mid-week dinners. The meals include slow cooker teriyaki ribs, cheesy rigatoni pepper bake and Tex-Mex meatballs. S21, Ep3
Osage Cowboy's Lunch. Ree makes a classic lunch for her husband and the cowboys. She brings them cheeseburger meatloaf, spicy green beans and grab-and-go cow patty cookies. S21, Ep4
With over-fishing now considered a major global crisis, chef Jamie Oliver is on a mission to convince home cooks and professionals to avoid common seafood and be more experimental.
Cooking With Giada. Ina invites Giada De Laurentiis round for a day of cooking together. They enjoy lobster and potato salad for lunch and then Giada makes a delicious gnocchi dish. S9, Ep6
Cooking With Tyler Florence. Ina has a cooking date with chef Tyler Florence. They bake a birthday cake together and visit the local farm scene to pick up vegetables and chicken. S9, Ep7
Victorian. Mary dusts off her antique cookbooks to find a technical recipe that dates back to the late 1800s, in an episode devoted entirely to the baking landscape of the Victorian era. S6, Ep7
Patisserie. It's the quarter-finals, and the five remaining bakers work their way through childhood favourites and delicate French fancies to a mountainous choux pastry masterpiece. S6, Ep8
Norfolk. James explores Norfolk with chef Galton Blackiston. They visit a fish and chip shop, which inspires James to whip up tempura monkfish on the beach. S1, Ep13
London And Bray. James visits Clare Smyth's restaurant Core in London. Plus, he cooks up an Indian-inspired dish of pigeon with split pea dhal. S1, Ep14
Rick Stein goes on a pigeon shoot in Cornwall, inspiring him to cook pigeon and peas. Plus, after visiting Devon, he makes a warm skate salad. S1, Ep1
Rick Stein cooks sautéed chicken with 40 cloves of garlic and a warm pheasant salad. Plus, he learns how cider is made at an old-fashioned cider farm in Somerset. S1, Ep2

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep4
Shift 1: Speed. In the first shift of 24 nonstop hours, 24 talented chefs take on three challenges testing speed to impress judge Antonia Lofaso. S2, Ep1
Triple D All Stars. Host Guy Fieri is on the road with a couple of his A-list friends: Matthew McConaughey and fellow foodie Andrew Zimmern. S14, Ep1
Streamlined Sammies. Host Guy Fieri is in Texas, where a tiny trailer serves up sandwiches to the bar next door. Then, Guy focuses on one of his favourite foods: burgers. S14, Ep2
Los Angeles, CA. Casey Webb is in Los Angeles to take on a massive burrito packed with every ingredient from the kitchen and soaked in a sauce 600 times hotter than a jalapeno. S2, Ep7
San Diego, CA. Casey Webb travels to San Diego, California in search of the city's best food. Plus, he takes on a monster 5.5-pound pancake stack with ham, steak, bacon and sausage. S2, Ep8
Naked Fishing. Things quickly get out-of-hand when Buddy fills an order for an ice-fishing cake. Meanwhile, an important client requests a naked cake and Buddy is not impressed. S7, Ep3
The Butcher and the Baker. The juices get flowing when Buddy and the team go for a consultation at a butchers and take a slab of inspiration home for lunch. S7, Ep4

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep4
Rick visits a free-range goose farm in Leicester, inspiring him to tackle a roast goose dish. Plus, he visits the oldest eel and pie shop in London. S1, Ep3
Rick experiences the Lake District's quality produce. He cooks a Chinese-inspired wild boar stew. Plus, he meets a farmer who keeps Herdwick sheep. S1, Ep4
Quarterback Training. Ree cooks a dinner for the hungry guys. Her recipes include ranch chicken taquitos with sour cream dip, hearty Mexican rice and beans and a delicious dessert. S21, Ep5
Chocolate Appreciation Day. Ree Drummond makes a Mississippi mud cake and white chocolate macadamia nut cookies. Plus, she shows how to make her easy chocolate tart. S21, Ep6
Celebration. Jamie makes an incredible celebration meal of rainbow tomato crostini and roast lamb, finished off with strawberry panna cottas and mango caipirinhas. S1, Ep1
Cooking With Michael Symon. Ina and Michael Symon cook a Greek feast. He makes delicious smoked pork ribs on the grill whilst she whips up the side dishes, like spicy hummus. S9, Ep8
Barefoot Chocolate Desserts. Ina makes her favourite chocolate desserts from the Barefoot Contessa hall of fame. She makes cupcakes with peanut butter frosting and chocolate gelato. S9, Ep9
Chocolate. Week nine, and that can only mean one thing, it's the semi-finals. The pressure is on as the bakers get to grips with one of the most difficult ingredients - chocolate. S6, Ep9
The Final. It's the final, and Mary and Paul are expecting nothing short of perfection. Who will hold their nerve and be crowned winner of this year's Great British Bake Off? S6, Ep10
Laverstoke And Goodwood. James visits his adopted home county of Hampshire. The countryside inspires him to cook up a hearty game dish with produce from the forest. S1, Ep15
Kent. James explores the county of Kent with chef and restaurateur Mark Sargeant. He makes grilled herring and bread and butter pudding. S1, Ep16

Los Angeles. Paul visits Hollywood for the very first time. He explores the Los Angeles baking scene. He meets locals and learns how to make some traditional baked goods. S2, Ep2
With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep4
Crankin' Up The Classics. Guy Fieri is in Minneapolis to check out an Italian deli that serves pizza and fresh pasta. In Dallas, he meets Ear Campbell for gumbo and burgers. S14, Ep3
Passin' The Baton. Guy Fieri is in California to check out a breakfast place that serves up pancake burritos. In Baltimore, he visits a Greek eatery with a history. S14, Ep4
